content: Just as deliberate structured reflection (reflexion) is taught as a core metacognitive skill to improve high school students’ long-term learning outcomes, autonomous AI agents can be designed to embed graded guided reflexion routines, similar to the scaffolded writing reflection exercises used in high school English curricula, to iteratively refine their task outputs over time. This structured, curriculum-inspired approach to reflexion outperforms ad-hoc self-reflection for narrow task learning in early autonomous agent prototypes.
